title: Engine ID
description: Closed enum of supported AEO engines. v1 = ChatGPT Search only, per CLAUDE.md Engine scope and ADR-0013:58.
$comment: compat_class: closed. Authority: approved plan §2 row 2; ruling R1 (docs/.../virtual-cooking-map.md §1.1) — engine_id is a single standalone file, NOT duplicated inside common/identifiers. ADR-0013:58 (docs/decisions/ADR-0013-event-envelope-v1.md) is the field-level authority; CLAUDE.md 'Engine scope (v1)' is the operating-principle authority (Google AI Overviews / Google AI Mode / Gemini disabled — optimize/observe/claim forbidden). Extension (adding another engine value) requires separate re-approval + a new/revised ADR + a major version bump (ADR-0012 enum-widening-is-major rule). Tolerant-read obligation is DEFERRED here because v1 is a single-value enum (nothing to be tolerant of yet); this is the SECOND recorded deferral (see ADR-0011/ADR-0012 tolerant-read obligation notes and virtual-cooking-map.md R11/R9 discussion) — the obligation transfers forward to the next multi-value enum contract introduced in this catalog.

engine_required_payload.$comment: Reusable activation fragment for P1 payload families (observation/citation/experiment events) that must carry a required engine_id field. Consumers compose this via allOf/$ref rather than redeclaring engine_id inline.
